params.onTrack: function(track: TrackState | null) -> void; called immediately with the current state (or null if unknown) and again on every change

fields.title: string (non-empty; the song title as reported by SMTC's MediaProperties.Title for the active Apple Music session)
fields.artist: string (possibly empty if SMTC reports no artist; the artist as reported by SMTC's MediaProperties.Artist)
fields.isPlaying: bool (true if the SMTC playback status for the Apple Music session is Playing; false for Paused/Stopped/Closed/Changing/Opened)
fields.sourceAppId: string (the SMTC SourceAppUserModelId of the session being read; for the Windows Apple Music app this is the Microsoft Store package family name, expected to contain 'AppleMusic' substring)

idleState().returns: TrackState | null — the well-defined idle state to be passed to onTrack when Apple Music is closed or has no current session. Implementation MUST return null in that case (not a fabricated track).

notes: Subscription is push-based. Source implementation handles polling vs event-driven internally (per IP6); consumers see only the callback. The source MUST coalesce rapid changes (debounce ~150ms) so the renderer is not flooded during track scrubbing. The source MUST filter the SMTC sessions to the Apple Music session by SourceAppUserModelId; if multiple media apps publish sessions concurrently, the Apple Music one is the target even when it is not GetCurrentSession().
